data.title: eToro jumps 30% on Nasdaq debut after upsized IPO
data.description:
Crypto and stock trading platform eToro has seen its share price gain nearly 30% during its debut on the Nasdaq after the company made a last-minute boost to its initial public offering. Shares in eToro Group Ltd (ETOR) closed May 14 trading at $67, up 28. 9% from its initial offering price of $52, according to Yahoo Finance. It brings the company’s market value to over $5
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/etoro-jumps-30-percent-nasdaq-debut-upsized-ipo?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Ukraine set to make Bitcoin history with first European strategic reserve
data.description:
Ukraine is drafting a law to create the first European Strategic Bitcoin Reserve.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ukraine-eyes-bitcoin-strategic-reserve/

data.title: Crypto startups scaring away VCs with 80x valuations: 10T Holdings
data.description:
Too many crypto startups are pricing themselves out of venture capital funding by chasing valuations far exceeding their revenues, according to Dan Tapeiro, the CEO of crypto-focused venture capital firm 10T Holdings. “For some reason, founders and CEOs think that they should be raising capital at 50 to 80 times revenue. So that makes it very hard for us to make a return for our liquidity providers,” Tapeiro said while speaking in a panel discussion at the Consensus conference in Toronto on May 14. “So a lot of those deals we just pass almost automatically, even businesses that we really like, we won't invest in if the price isn't reasonable in the beginning
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-startup-80x-valuations-push-away-vc-funding?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Where’s Next Major Dogecoin Resistance? On-Chain Data Points To This
data.description:
On-chain data suggests this upcoming Dogecoin level could prove to be a resistance wall based on the investor cost basis distribution. A Large Amount Of Dogecoin Was Last Purchased At $0. 36 In a new post on X, analyst Ali Martinez has talked about Dogecoin levels that appear significant on the UTXO Realized Price Distribution (URPD) of the memecoin. The “URPD” refers to an on-chain indicator that basically tells us about how much of the asset’s supply was last purchased at which price levels
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/dogecoin/dogecoin-resistance-on-chain-data-points-this/

data.title: Brazil’s central bank proposes strict regulation of stablecoin transfers
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily reported that several Brazilian government sources familiar with the plan revealed that Brazilian government officials are considering issuing the country's first sovereign bond denominated in RMB. Currently, this move is still under consideration and no final decision has been made. A potential plan to issue yuan-denominated sovereign bonds would be a notable development in Brazil’s financial strategy.
Meanwhile, Brazil’s central bank has proposed strict regulation of stablecoin transfers as part of a new cryptocurrency regulatory framework. The proposed rules include restrictions on sending stablecoins to wallets not controlled by Brazilian entities, reflecting the country’s tightening stance on cryptocurrency trading within the country. (Defiant)

data.title: Potential Delay in U.S. Crypto Regulation Reform Warned by Legal Expert
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, cryptocurrency advocate and attorney John E. Deaton has issued a warning that substantial crypto regulatory reform in the United States could be postponed until 2029 if Congress fails to pass the GENIUS stablecoin bill promptly. This perspective is supported by Messari founder Ryan Selkis.

Deaton criticized the U.S. regulatory approach, highlighting its drastic shifts with changes in government. He noted that current regulations still rely on legal frameworks from the 1930s and 1940s. Selkis emphasized that if the bill is not passed this week, crypto reform may face significant setbacks during the current term of U.S. President Donald Trump.

data.title: Crypto fintech company Antalpha's U.S. stock price surged 70% on its first day of listing, triggering a circuit breaker
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to GlobeNewswire, Antalpha Platform Holding Company announced today that its initial public offering (IPO) was priced at US$12.80 per common share, with a total issuance of 3,850,000 common shares. The company's common stock has been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Market and is expected to begin trading on May 14, 2025, with the stock code "ANTA". The offering is expected to be completed on May 15, 2025, subject to customary delivery conditions. It is reported that Antalpha is a crypto fintech company that focuses on providing liquidity and risk management solutions for institutional Bitcoin miners. As a major lending partner of Bitmain, Antalpha provides supply chain lending and margin lending services through its Antalpha Prime technology platform, enabling customers to initiate and manage digital asset loans while monitoring collateral positions through near real-time data. According to market data, as of press time, the share price of Antalpha (ANTA) has risen to US$22.2, with an intraday increase of 73.59%, triggering a circuit breaker during trading.

data.title: Metaplanet releases Q1 financial report: Thanks to Bitcoin reserve strategy, the company's quarterly performance is the best in history
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Metaplanet, a Japanese listed company, released its Q1 financial report this morning. The financial report pointed out that Metaplanet achieved the best performance in the company's history this quarter, with revenue of US$6.139 million, an increase of 8% month-on-month and a surge of 943% year-on-year, and profit of US$4.151 million, an increase of 11% month-on-month, of which 88% of the revenue came from Bitcoin option sales.
In addition, the company currently holds a total of 6,796 Bitcoins, with a goal of reaching a reserve of 10,000 Bitcoins by the end of the year.

data.title: Lawyer: If the US Congress fails to pass the GENIUS Act in time, crypto regulatory reform will be delayed until 2029
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: John E Deaton, a lawyer who supports cryptocurrencies, warned that if the US Congress fails to pass the stablecoin GENIUS Act in time, substantive crypto regulatory reforms may be delayed until 2029. This view is supported by Messari founder Ryan Selkis and others. Deaton criticized the US regulatory attitude for swinging dramatically between government changes, and the current regulation still relies on the legal framework of the 1930s and 1940s. Selkis specifically pointed out that if the bill is not passed this week, crypto reforms may die during the Trump administration in the future. (Coinpedia)

data.title: Bipartisan Senators Near Consensus on GENIUS Act
data.description: According to BlockBeats, a bipartisan group of U.S. senators is nearing a consensus on the text of the GENIUS Act, although the content has not been finalized. The Senate is currently discussing the resumption of the bill's review process, aiming to complete the vote before the Memorial Day recess. However, progress depends on reaching a procedural agreement with the Democratic Party.

data.title: Nebraska passes new bill to make Bitcoin mining more difficult
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The Nebraska State Legislature just passed a bill to regulate Bitcoin miners. LB 526 requires large miners to bear the cost of infrastructure upgrades, requires miners to report energy usage, and allows authorities to interrupt service. The bill was passed unanimously with a vote of 49:0. 
Nebraska's Bitcoin mining bill LB 526 is not a huge setback for companies in the state. The bill is just over two pages long and is very straightforward. Unlike the anti-mining bill, it does not involve carbon emissions or other environmental impacts. Instead, it focuses almost entirely on the power grid.

data.title: Nebraska Legislature Passes Bitcoin Mining Regulation Bill
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Nebraska Legislature has unanimously passed a bill regulating Bitcoin miners. The LB 526 bill requires large-scale miners to cover infrastructure upgrade costs, report energy usage, and grants authorities the power to interrupt services. The bill was approved with a 49:0 vote.

The LB 526 Bitcoin mining bill is not considered a significant setback for companies in Nebraska. The legislation is concise, spanning just over two pages, and is straightforward in its approach. Unlike anti-mining bills, it does not address carbon emissions or other environmental impacts, focusing instead almost entirely on the electrical grid.

data.title: Russian Power Engineers Develop New Solutions to Hunt Illegal Crypto Miners
data.description:
Russian power engineers say they have developed new methods to help them track down and prosecute illegal crypto miners operating in areas prone to electricity shortages.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/russian-power-engineers-develop-new-solutions-to-hunt-illegal-crypto-miners/

Pump. fun is no longer untouchable, and it now risks fading into obscurity as it has to […]
